questions.question: The volume of a cone of radius 13 cm is 507π cm3. Find its height.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 9 cm. Volume of cone = 1/3 × π × (13)2 × h ⇒ 507π = (1/3) × π × 169 × h ⇒ h = (507 × 3)/169 ⇒ h = 1521/169 = 9 cm

questions.question: If a + b + c = 6 and a2 + b2 + c2 = 14, then what is the value of (a - b)2 + (b − c)2 + (c- a)2?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 6. We know that, (a + b + c)2 = a2 + b2 + c2 + 2(ab + bc + ca) (6)2 = 14 + 2(ab + bc + ca) ab + bc + ca = 11 Now (a - b)2 + (b − c)2 + (c- a)2 = a2 + b2 - 2ab + b2 + c2 - 2bc+ c2 + a2 - 2ac = 2(a2 + b2 + c2) - 2(ab + bc + ca) = 2(14) - 2(11) = 28 - 22 = 6

questions.question: Find the number of diagonals of a regular polygon, sum of whose interior angles is 2700°.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 119. The sum of the interior angles of a regular polygon = (n - 2) × 180° ⇒ (n - 2) × 180° = 2700° ⇒ n - 2 = 270/18 = 15 n = 17 Sides of a regular polygon (n) = 17 The number of diagonals of a regular polygon = n (n - 3)/2 = 17 × (17 - 3)/2 = 17 × 7 = 119

questions.question: Two successive discounts, each of x% on the marked price of an article, are equal to a single discount of Rs. 331.20. If the marked price of the article is Rs. 920, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 20% 1st discount = x% of 920 = Rs. 9.2x Have to pay amount Rs. (920 – 9.2x) 2nd discount x% of Rs (920 - 9.2x) = (920 – 9.2x)x/100 Accordingly, 9.2x + x(920 – 9.2x)/100 = 331.20 ⇒ 9200x + 9200x – 92x2 = 331200 ⇒ 92x2 – 18400x + 331200 = 0 ⇒ x2 – 200x + 3600 = 0 ⇒ x2 – 180x - 20x + 3600 = 0 ⇒ (x – 180)(x – 20) = 0 x = 180 or x = 20 180% invalid as per the question.

questions.question: What is the area (in m2, up to 1 place of decimal) of an equilateral triangular field of side 8.5 m?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 18.1\(\sqrt{3}\). Area of equilateral triangle = √3/4 a² (where a is the side of the equilateral triangle) Area of triangle = (√3/4) × (8.5)² ⇒ (√3/4) × 72.25 ⇒ √3 × (18.06) ⇒ 18.1 √3

questions.question: The value of \(\frac{5}{4} \times 2\frac{2}{3} \div \frac{5}{9}\;of\;\;1\frac{1}{5} + \frac{2}{{25}} \times 4\frac{1}{6} \div \frac{2}{7}\;of\;2\frac{1}{3}\) is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 5 \(\frac{1}{2}\) Evaluate using the BODMAS rule, which says 'Brackets, Order, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction'. \(\frac{5}{4} \times 2\frac{2}{3} \div \frac{5}{9}\;of\;\;1\frac{1}{5} + \frac{2}{{25}} \times 4\frac{1}{6} \div \frac{2}{7}\;of\;2\frac{1}{3}\) = \(\frac{5}{4} \times \frac{8}{3} \div \frac{5}{9}\;of\;\;\frac{6}{5} + \frac{2}{{25}} \times \frac{25}{6} \div \frac{2}{7}\;of\;\frac{7}{3}\) =\(\frac{5}{4} \times \frac{8}{3} \times \frac{9}{5}\ \times \;\frac{6}{5} + \frac{2}{{25}} \times \frac{25}{6} \times \frac{7}{2}\ \times \;\frac{7}{3}\) = 5 \(\frac{1}{2}\)

questions.question: PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral. If ∠P is three times of ∠R and ∠S is four times of ∠Q, then the sum of ∠S + ∠R will be:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 189°. Given that PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral. As we know that ⇒ ∠P + ∠R = 180º ⇒ 3∠R + ∠R = 180º ∠R = 45º ⇒ ∠Q + ∠S = 180º ⇒ ∠Q + 4 ∠Q = 180º ⇒ 5 ∠Q = 180º ∠Q = 36º ⇒ ∠S = 4 × 36 = 144º ∠S + ∠R = 144º + 45º = 189º

questions.question: Varun, Ragav, and Nagesh enter into a partnership. Varun contributes one-third of the whole capital, while Ragav contributes as much as Varun and Nagesh contribute. If the profit at the end of the year is Rs. 1,02,000, how much would Nagesh receive? (In Rs.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 17000. Given that: Varun contributes one-third of the whole capital . Ragav contributes as much as Varun and Nagesh together contribute . Let Nagesh's invested capital be x . Profit ∝ Investment × time Varun's investment = 102000/3 = Rs. 34000 Raghav's investment = 34000 + x According to the question: ⇒ 34000 + (34000 + x) + x = 102000 ⇒ 68000 + 2x = 102000 ⇒ 2x = 102000 - 68000 = 34000 ⇒ x = 17000 Hence, Nagesh's investment is Rs. 17000.

questions.question: A batsman makes a score of 97 runs in the 15th innings and thus increases his average by 5. Find his average after the 15th inning.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 27. Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations Let x be the batsman's average after 14 innings. The total runs scored by the batsman in 14 innings = 14x According to the question - ⇒ 14x + 97 = 15(x + 5) ⇒ 14x + 97 = 15x + 75 ⇒ x = 22 Average after 15th innings = x + 5 = 5 + 22 = 27

questions.question: Raju and Raman can complete a piece of work in 12 days and 16 days, respectively. If they work on alternate days, starting with Raju, in how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 13\(\frac{2}{3}\)days. Raju and Raman can complete a piece of work in 12 days and 16 days L.C.M(12 and 16) = 48 The efficiency of Raju = 48/12 = 4 units The efficiency of Raman = 48/16 = 3 units Starting from Raju Raju's work on the first day = 4 units, Raman's work on the second day = 3 units Total work in 2 days is 7 units So, in 12 days they will complete 42 units Next day Raju will work 4 units Completed work = 42 + 4 = 46 The next day Raman will work 3 units but the remaining work is only 2 units, so he will do 2/3 units Thus, Total days = 12 + 1 + 2/3 = 13 \(\frac{2}{3}\)days.

questions.question: The following figure shows the production of mobiles in lakh units of four cities in different years. Study the figure and answer the question that follows. What is the ratio between the average production in Delhi and Kolkata during the period of 2019 to 2022?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 11 : 9. The average production (in lakhs) in Delhi from 2019 to 2022 = (600 + 450 + 350 + 800)/4 = 2200/4 = 550 The average production (in lakhs) in Kolkata from 2019 to 2022 = (500 + 400 + 250 + 650)/4 = 1800/4 = 450 Required ratio = 550 : 450 = 11 : 9 Therefore, the ratio between the average production in Delhi and Kolkata during the period of 2019 to 2022 is 11 : 9.

questions.question: Find the missing frequency(p) for the following distribution, whose mean is 8: X: 3 5 7 9 11 13 f: 6 8 15 p 8 4
Find the remainder when 413 divided by 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1. 413 ÷ 3 Remainder = ? ⇒ 413/3 = (3 + 1)13/3 = 1/3 ⇒ Remainder = 1

questions.question: What is 30% of 40% of 150% of 200% of 360?

Subir claimed to sell his items at only 5% above the cost of production, but used a weight that had 1 kg written on it, though it actually weighed 960 grams. What was the actual profit percentage earned by Subir?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 9.375%. According to the question, Let the cost price of item be Rs. 1000, then Marked Up Price = 5% of CP + CP ⇒ MP = 1000 (1 + 5%) ⇒ MP = 1000 × 1.05 = Rs. 1050 But he Sold 960g instead of 1kg for Rs. 1050, Then CP of 960g = Rs. 960 ⇒ Profit% = (1050 - 960)/960 × 100 ⇒ Profit% = 90/960 × 100 = 9.375%

questions.question: Which of the following is divisible by 24?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 2856. The divisibility rule of 24: A number is divisible by 24 if it is divisible by 3 and 8. The divisibility rule of 3: if the sum of digits of a number is a multiple of 3, the number will be completely divisible by 3. The divisibility rule of 8: if the last three numbers are zero or divisible by 8, the whole number is divisible by 8. ⇒ Option 1 = 2940 The sum of digits = 2 + 9 + 4 + 0 = 15 [So, divisible by 3] The last 3 digits = 940/8 = 117.5 [Not divisible by 8] So, 2940 is not divisible by 24 ⇒ Option 2 = 2856 The sum of digits = 2 + 8 + 5 + 6 = 21 [So, divisible by 3] The last 3 digits = 856/8 = 107 [ divisible by 8] So, 2940 is divisible by 24 ⇒ Option 3 = 1258 The sum of digits = 1 + 2 + 5 + 8 = 16 [So, not divisible by 3] The last 3 digits = 258/8 = 32.25 [Not divisible by 8] So, 2940 is not divisible by 24 ⇒ Option 4 = 1860 The sum of digits = 1 + 8 + 6 + 0 = 15 [So, divisible by 3] The last 3 digits = 860/8 = 107.5 [Not divisible by 8] So, 2940 is not divisible by 24 Hence, only 2856 is divisible by 24

questions.question: It is given that ∆ABC ≅ ∆PQR, AB = 5 cm, ∠B = 40°, and ∠A = 80°. Which of the following options is true?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. PQ = 5 cm and ∠R = 60°. AB = PQ = 5cm ∠B = ∠Q = 40º ∠A = ∠P = 80° We know that ⇒ ∠P + ∠Q + ∠R = 180º ⇒∠P = 180º - 40º - 80º = 60º

questions.question: If (x + \(\frac{1}{x}\)) = \(\frac{11}{5}\), what is the value of (x3 + \(\frac{1}{x^3}\))?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 4\(\frac{6}{125}\) \(\begin{aligned} & x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^3-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\left(\frac{11}{5}\right)^3-3\left(\frac{11}{5}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\frac{1331}{125}-\frac{33}{5} \\ & \Rightarrow x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=\frac{506}{125} \\ & \Rightarrow x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}=4 \frac{6}{125}\end{aligned}\)

questions.question: What is the H.C.F. of 2.88, 1.76, 0.16?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 4/25. The given numbers are 2.88, 1.76, 0.16. ⇒ 288/100, 176/100, 16/100 H.C.F. of fractions = H.C.F. of numerator/L.C.M. of the denominator ⇒ H.C.F. of 288, 176, 16 = 16 ⇒ L.C.M. of 100, 100, 100 = 100 Hence, 16/100 = 4/25

questions.question: A person covers 11 km with a speed of 7 km/h, 25 km with a speed of 10 km/h and 30 km with a speed of 20 km/h. What is the average speed of the person?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(11\frac{11}{13}\) km/h. ⇒ Total Distance = 11 + 25 + 30 = 66 km ⇒ Total Time = \(11 \over 7\)+ \(25 \over 10\)+ \(30 \over20\)= \(11 \over 7\)+ \(5 \over 2\)+ \(3 \over 2\)= \(\frac{39}{7}\) hours ⇒ Average speed = \(Total\,Distance \over Total \, Time\)= \(\frac{66}{​39 \over 7} \)= 66 × \(\frac{7}{39}\) = \(\frac{154}{13}\) = \(11\frac{11}{13}\) km/h.

questions.question: Two points A and B are on the ground and on opposite sides of a tower. A is closer to the foot of tower by 42m than B. If the angles of elevation of the top of the tower, as observed from A and B. are 60°and 45° respectively, then the height of the tower is closest to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 99.4 m. In ∆MAN tan60° =\( \frac{x+42}{x}\) \(\sqrt3x\)–x=42 x=\(\frac{42}{\sqrt3-1}\times\frac{\sqrt3+1}{\sqrt3+1}\) x=\(21\left(\sqrt3+1\right) \) x=\(21\left(1.732+1\right) \) x=\(21\times2.732=57.372 \) Height of tower = x+42 = 57.372+42 = 99.372

questions.question: A farmer has 10 Parrots and 40 horses. Find the average number of legs per animal.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 3.6. Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations Now, according to the question, The farmer has a total of animals = 10 + 40 = 50 A parrot has 2 legs and a horse has 4 legs, The total number of legs = (2 × 10) + (4 × 40) = 180 The average number of legs per animal = 180/50 = 3.6

questions.question: Working 12 hours per day, Raj can complete a work in 12 days. How many hours per day he should work so as to finish the same work in 9 days?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 16 hours. Given that Working 12 hours per day, Raj can complete a work in 12 days Let the time taken by Raj to complete the work in 9 days be H According to the question; ⇒ 12 × 12 = 9 × H ⇒ 144 = 9 × H ⇒ D = 144/9 = 16 hours

questions.question: What is the rate of compound interest, if the interest on ₹ 10,000 for 2 years is ₹ 609, when interest is compounded annually?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 3% per annum. Principal = 10000 Rate = R Time = 2 years CI = 609 Amount = P(1 + R/100)T ----- (1) Where, P = principal, R = rate and T = time Using equation (1), we get Amount = 10000 + 609 = 10609 ⇒ 10609/10000 = (1 + R/100)2 ⇒ 103/100 = 1 + R/100 ⇒ 3/100 = R/100 ⇒ R = 3%

questions.question: Three friends A, B, and C invested in a business in a ratio of 2 : 3 : 4. After 6 months, C withdrew half his capital. If the total profit earned for the year is Rs.110000 then find the profit earned by C. (In Rs.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 41250. Let the investments of A, B, and C = 2x, 3x, and 4x According to the question The ratio of the profits of A : B : C ⇒ (2x × 12) : (3x × 12) : (4x × 6 + 2x × 6) ⇒ 24x : 36x : (24x + 12x) ⇒ 2 : 3 : 3 Hence, the profit earned by C is (3/8) × 110000 = Rs. 41250.

questions.question: In an election, 2% of the people enrolled in the voter list did not participate, and 500 votes were invalid. Two candidates, A and B, fought the election, and A defeated B by 200 votes. If 43% of the people enrolled in the voter list casted their votes in favor of A, then what is the total number of casted votes?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 2450. Calculations: Let the total votes be x 2% of the people enrolled in the voter list did not participate = 98% of x = 0.98x 500 votes are invalid, then total votes (0.98x - 500) The winning candidate gets 43% of the total votes = 0.43x Losing candidate gets = 0.43x - 200 According to the question; ⇒ 0.43x + 0.43x - 200 = 0.98x - 500 ⇒ 0.86x = 0.98x - 300 ⇒ 0.12x = 30000 ⇒ x = 2500 Hence, the number of total casted votes = 98% of 2500 = 2450

questions.question: The ratio of money with Jack and John is 7 : 13, and that with John and Jonas is 9 : 11. If Jack has Rs 6,300, then how much money does Jonas have?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs 14,300. Ratio of Jack and John = 7/13 = (7 × 9)/(13 × 9) = 63/117 Ratio of John and Jonas = 9/11 = (9 × 13)/(11 × 13) = 117/143 Ratio (Jack : John : Jonas) = 63 : 117 : 143 Let the amount of money Jack has be 63x. According to the question; ⇒ 63x = 6300 ⇒ x = 100 Money with Jonas = (143 × 100) = Rs. 14,300

questions.question: The difference between the compound interest, when compounded annually, and the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 2 years at 4.2% is ₹8.82. What is the sum?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. ₹5,000. The difference between SI and CI for a certain amount of money at a particular rate of interest = {Principal × Rate2}/1002 ⇒ 8.82 = {P × 4.2 × 4.2}/1002 ⇒ 88200 = P × 17.64 ⇒ P = 10000/2 ⇒ P = 5000

questions.question: A mixture contains alcohol and water in the ratio of 12 : 5. On adding 14 liters of water, the ratio of alcohol to water becomes 4 : 3. The quantity of alcohol in the mixture is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 42L. The initial ratio of alcohol to water = 12 : 5 Let the quantity of alcohol be 12x and the quantity of water be 5x. So, 12x/(5x + 14) = 4/3 ⇒ 36x = 20x + 56 ⇒ 36x - 20x = 56 ⇒ 16x = 56 ⇒ x = 56/16 = 3.5 ⇒ 12x = 3.5 × 12 = 42 Therefore, the quantity of alcohol in the mixture is 42L.

questions.question: A pond is 50 m long, 35 m wide and 2.5 m deep. Find the capacity of the pond.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 4375 m3. The pond will be in the shape of a cuboid. Thus the volume of the cuboid = length × breadth × height ⇒ Volume = 50 × 35 × 2.5 = 4375 cm3

questions.question: A person sold an article at a loss of 12%. Had he sold it at a gain of 10.5%, he would have got Rs. 112.50 more. What is the original selling price (in Rs.) of the article?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 440.00 Selling Price = Cost Price × (1 - loss%/100) Selling Price = Cost Price × (1 + Profit%/100) Let the cost price of the article be Rs. 100P. So, his actual selling price = 100P × 0.88 = 88P Now, had he sold it at a gain of 10.5%, his selling price would have been = 100P × 1.105 = 110.5P Given, 110.5P - 88P = 112.5 ⇒ P = 5 ⇒ 88P = 440

questions.question: Ramesh starts from a place J and reaches the place K in 28 hours. He travels 2/5 distance at the speed of 10 km/hr and the remaining distance at the speed of 20 km/hr. What is the distance between J and K?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 400 km. Let the total distance be x Speed = Distance/Time Time taken by Ramesh to cover 2/5 of the distance = (2x/5)/10 Time taken by Ramesh to cover 3/5 of the distance = (3x/5)/20 ⇒ (2x/5)/10 + (3x/5)/20 = 28 ⇒ 2x/50 + 3x/100 = 28 ⇒ 4x/100 + 3x/100 = 28 ⇒ 7x = 2800 ⇒ x = 400 km

questions.question: If tanθ = \(2 \over \sqrt{11}\), 0° \(\frac{2cosec²θ - 3sec²θ}{3cosec²θ + 4sec²θ}\) is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. \(10 \over 49\) \(\frac{2cosec²θ - 3sec²θ}{3cosec²θ + 4sec²θ}\) Convert into sin & cos =\({2cos²θ - 3sin²θ} \over {3cos²θ + 4sin²θ}\) Divide by cos²θ =\({2 - 3tan²θ} \over {3 + 4tan²θ}\) = \(\frac{2-3 ({\frac{2}{\sqrt{11}}})^2}{3+4 (\frac{2}{\sqrt{{11}}})^2} \) = \(\frac{2 - \frac{12}{11}}{3 + \frac{16}{11}} =\frac{\frac{10}{11}}{\frac{49}{11}} = \frac{10}{49}\)

questions.question: A and B undertake a project worth ₹36,000. A alone can do the work in 25 days. They worked together for 5 days. For the next five days, B worked alone. After that, substituted B and completed the remaining work in 5 days. The share of A in the earnings is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Rs. 14,400. Total work = Time × Efficiency According to the question ⇒ (A + B) × 5 + B × 5 + A × 5 = A × 25 ⇒ 2A + 2B = 5A ⇒ 3A = 2B ⇒ A/B = 2/3 Hence, the share of A in the earnings = (2/5) × 36000 ⇒ Rs. 14400

questions.question: Amit told Shweta, "To reach our apartment, your mother must walk 560 m towards the east from her restaurant. She must then take a right turn and walk 543 m. She must take another right turn and walk 560 m. Then she must take one final right turn and walk 120 m". How far and in which direction is the apartment from the restaurant? (All turns are 90 degree turns only)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 423 m, South. The apartment from the restaurant is 423m and in the South direction.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially replace the * signs and to balance the given equation. 30 * 5 * 3 * 6 * 15 * 9
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. ÷, +, ×, -, =. Option 4: ÷, +, ×, -, = (30 ÷ 5) + 3 × 6 - 15 = 9 ⇒ 6 + (3 × 6) - 15 = 9 ⇒ (6 + 18) - 15 ⇒ 24 - 15 ⇒ 9 is equal to 9.

questions.question: Out of the given options, three are similar in a certain manner. However, one option is NOT like the other three. Select the option which is different from the rest.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. EH28. Logic: (Positional value of 1st letter + Positional value of 2nd letter) × 2 = Number Except EH28, all follow the same logic. While EH28 → (5 + 8) × 2 = 26 ≠ 28

questions.question: The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if each of the letters in the word ‘CITIZEN’ is rearranged in the English alphabetical order from left to right?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Two. Given word: C I T I Z E N Alphabetical order: C E I I N T Z Thus, the position of the two letters (C, I) will remain unchanged.

questions.question: Statements: S > F = Z > N V > A ≥ U = H Conclusions: I. V > Z II. A > H III. A = H
options: Either conclusion II or III is true
Neither conclusion I nor II is true
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Either conclusion II or III is true. I. V > Z: False (S > F = Z > N V > A ≥ U = H) II. A > H: False (S > F = Z > N V > A ≥ U = H) III. A = H: False (S > F = Z > N V > A ≥ U = H) Conclusion II and III form complementary pairs, so Either Conclusion II or III follows.

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some sun is earth. All earth are planets. Some earth is sky. Conclusions: (I) Some planets are sky. (II) No sun is a sky.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusion (I) follows. The least possible Venn Diagram is shown below: Conclusions: (I) Some planets are sky. → Follows (As some earth is sky and all earth are planets, so, some planets are also sky) (II) No sun is a sky. → Does not follow (It is possible but not definite)

questions.question: Read the following information and answer the question that follows. (i) Five ladies Simran, Vaishali, Namita, Preeti, and Bhawna meet in a hotel for a party. They all sit around a circular table facing the centre of the table. (ii) Bhawna is sitting to the right of Vaishali. (iii) Simran is sitting to the left of Preeti. (iv) Preeti is sitting between Namita and Simran. Who is sitting to the immediate right of Namita?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Vaishali. Vaishali is sitting to the immediate right of Namita.

questions.question: Which of the following could be the code for ‘Several’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. cal. Thus, the code for ‘Several’ is 'cal'.

questions.question: A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. B sits second to the right of D. A is an immediate neighbour of E and C. E sits second to the left of F. What is the position of F with respect to B?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. immediate left. According to the information, the arrangement is: Thus, F is immediate left of B.

questions.question: Which letter clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? BNS, COT, DPU, EQV, ?

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘A + B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’, ‘A – B’ means ‘A is the father of B’, ‘A $ B’ means ‘A is the son of B’, ‘A % B’ means ‘A is the sister of B’, ‘A / B’ means ‘A is the wife of B’ and ‘A * B’ means ‘A is the husband of B’. Which of the following means V is W's son's wife? (i) V % T / W – S * U (ii) T * U % W + S / V (iii) S $ U / W – T * V (iv) S + U $ T – W * V
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Only (iii). S $ U / W – T * V → S is the son of U, U is the wife of W, W is the father of T, T is the husband of V. Thus, V is W's son's wife.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs replaces __ in series given? B4U, F12P, __ , N108F
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. J36K. B + 4 = F, F + 4 = J, J + 4 = N 4 × 3 = 12, 12 × 3 = 36, 36 × 3 = 108 U - 5 = P, P - 5 = K, K - 5 = F

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the given statements Statements: No charger is internet. Some charger are mobile. Some tablets are internet. Conclusions: I. No mobile is internet. II. No charger is tablet.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Neither conclusion (I) nor (II) follows. The least possible Venn diagram is as follows: Conclusions: I. No mobile is internet. → Does not follow (It is possible but not definite) II. No charger is tablet. → Does not follow (It is possible but not definite)

questions.question: Directions: In the question, two statements are given, these statements may be either independent causes or may be effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of these statements may be the effect of the other statements. Read both statements and decide which of the following answer choices correctly depicts the relationship between these two statements. I: The fast-paced and demanding life today can often lead to stressful situations. II: The number of suicide cases among teenagers is increasing.
options: If both the statements I and II are effects of same causes
If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect. The stressful situations mentioned in Statement I could be a contributing cause to the increase in teenage suicides mentioned in Statement II. So, Statement I is the cause, and Statement II is a possible effect of it.

questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, and F were six friends playing games around a circular table. They were standing facing the center of the table. E was standing to the immediate left of A. C was second to the left of F. There were exactly two people between D and A. B was at the immediate right of A. Which friend was at the immediate right of F?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. E. 1. E was standing to the immediate left of A. B was at the immediate right of A. 2. There were exactly two people between D and A. C was second to the left of F.

questions.question: Select the number from the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 30, 45, 75, 135, 255, ?

‘PVBT’ is related to ‘YSWE’ in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, ‘EKYM’ is related to ‘NHPB’. To which of the following is ‘WHLR’ related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. KZUO. ‘PVBT’ is related to ‘YSWE’ ‘EKYM’ is related to ‘NHPB' Similarly, ‘WHLR’ is related:

questions.question: How many times the hour hand and the minute hand coincide in a clock between 10:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. (same day)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 3 times. Time duration between 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. = 4 hours As, we know that clock hands coincide once in each hour. However, between 11 O' clock and 1 O' clock, they coincide just once, that is, at 12 O' clock. So, 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. the clock hands will coincide 3 times.

questions.question: Six students, A, B, C, D, E and F, participated in one of the International Mathematics Olympiad. Each of them scored different marks. C scored more than D but less than B. E scored less than A. B scored more than A but less than F. C scored less than E. Who scored the maximum marks among the given students?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. F. F > B > A > E > C > D Hence, F scored the maximum marks.

questions.question: ‘Float’ is related to ‘Dive’ in the same way as ‘Captivity’ is related to ‘________’. (The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Freedom. Float and Dive are antonyms. Float means to move slowly through air or water. Dive means to jump into water. Similarly, Captivity and Freedom are antonyms. Captivity means the situation in which a person or animal is kept somewhere and is not allowed to leave. Freedom means the state of not being held prisoner or controlled by somebody else.

questions.question: Each of A, B, W, V, N, M, and P went for a picnic on a different day of the week starting on Monday and ending on Sunday of the same week. Only one person went between M and V. M went on Wednesday. P went on the day immediately after B. Only one person has gone between W and B. B went on Thursday. A has gone just before Wednesday. On which day did P go for a picnic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Friday. Person Day V Monday A Tuesday M Wednesday B Thursday P Friday W Saturday N Sunday So, P has an exam on Friday.

questions.question: How many such pairs of digits are there in the number "247935689", each of which has as many digits between them in the number as they have between them in the number series?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Five. Pairs formed: (5,6) (8,9) (6,9)

questions.question: A mother is now three times as old as her daughter. 5 years ago, she was four times as old as her daughter. What is the current age of the daughter?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 15 years. Let the current age of the daughter be x years. Then, the mother's current age will be 3x years (as the mother is three times as old as her daughter). 5 years ago: Daughter's age: x - 5 Mother's age: 3x - 5 It is given that five years ago, the mother was four times as old as the daughter. Hence, 3x − 5 = 4(x − 5) Solving the equation: 3x − 5 = 4x − 20 3x - 4x = 5 - 20 x = 15 So, the current age of the daughter is 15 years.

questions.question: Three of the following four options are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does NOT belong to that group? (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
If the 17th day of the month falls on the 4th day before Wednesday, then what will be the day of the week on the 1st of that month?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Thursday. Given: The 17th day of the month falls on the 4th day before Wednesday So, 17th + 4 = 21th of the month will be Wednesday A day will repeat itself after seven days which means the 14th and 7th of the month is Wednesday. Hence, the 7th of the month (Wednesday) - 6 days = 1st (Thursday).

questions.question: If in a certain language, 'HISTORICAL' is coded as “6139581247”. How is 'HOLISTIC' coded in that code?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 65713912. 'HISTORICAL' is coded as “6139581247”: H I S T O R I C A L 6 1 3 9 5 8 1 2 4 7 So, ‘HOLISTIC’ would be coded as ‘65713912’.

questions.question: Directions: A question is given followed by two statements labeled I and II. Identify which of the statements is sufficient to answer the question. Who is the shortest of all? I. Sachin is taller than Mohan and shorter than Vipin. II. Devika is shorter than Gangan.
options: Both the statements I and II are insufficient to answer the question.
Either statement I alone or statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question.
Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question, while statement I alone is not sufficient to answer the question.
Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question, while statement II alone is not sufficient to answer the question.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Both statements I and II are insufficient to answer the question. I. Sachin is taller than Mohan and shorter than Vipin. (Vipin > Sachin > Mohan) II. Devika is shorter than Gangan. (Gangan > Devika) I and II: There is no relation between both statements so both statements I and II are insufficient to answer the question.

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged in the following equation to make it correct? 33 × 3 + 10 ÷ 2 ­- 13 = 18
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. × and ÷ Given equation: 33 × 3 + 10 ÷ 2 ­- 13 = 18 By interchanging × and ÷ signs, we get (33 ÷ 3) + (10 × 2) -­ 13 = (11 + 20) - 13 = 31 - 13 = 18

questions.question: In this question, three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All tower are pole. Some pole are light. Some light are bulb. Conclusions: I. Some light are pole. II. Some pole are tower. III. No light is bulb.
options: Only conclusions I and II follow.
Only conclusions II and III follow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Only conclusions I and II follow. The least possible Venn diagram is as follows Conclusions: I. Some pole are tower. → Follows (As all tower are pole. So, some pole are definitely tower.) II. Some light are pole. → Follows (As some pole are light. So, some light are pole also) III. No light is bulb. → Does not follow (it is given that some light are bulb. So, no light is bulb is false)

questions.question: Direction: In the given statement, the relationship between different elements is shown and it is followed by two conclusions. Choose the correct answer based on the information given below. Statement: 23 ≥ 24 > 25 ≤ 26 Conclusions: I. 23 > 26 II. 27 > 25
options: Either Conclusion I or II follows
Both Conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Only Conclusion II follows . I. 23 > 26: False (23 ≥ 24 > 25 ≤ 26 II. 27 > 25: True (23 ≥ 24 > 25 ≤ 26 = 28 → 25

questions.question: In a row of people, all facing north, Bunty is 8th from the left end. Sneha is 15th from the left end. Sneha is exactly between Bunty and Nupur. If Nupur is 7th from the right end of the line, how many people are there in the row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 28. If Nupur is 7th from the right end, and the total number of people is N, Nupur's position from the left end will be = N - 7 + 1 = N - 6 Distance from Bunty to Sneha = Distance from Sneha to Nupur ⇒ 15 − 8 = (N − 6) − 15 ⇒ 7 = N − 21 ⇒ N = 28

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘QWAL’ is written as ‘108’. How will ‘TPZ' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 81. Logic: No. of letters × 27 QWAL = 4 × 27 = 108 Similarly, TPZ = ? = 3 × 27 = 81

questions.question: India will host the Asian Open Short Track Speed Skating Trophy 2025 for the first time in which city?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Dehradun. India will host the Asian Open Short Track Speed Skating Trophy 2025 for the first time from August 20–23 at Maharana Pratap Sports College, Raipur, Dehradun. Over 11 Asian countries, including China, Japan, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand, Chinese Taipei, Vietnam, Malaysia, the Philippines, and India, will participate. Athletes will compete in nine events ranging from the 222-meter sprint to the 5000-meter relay. This historic event marks India’s debut in organizing a major international winter sports competition, promoting sports development and regional collaboration.

questions.question: Where was ISRO’s first Integrated Air Drop Test (IADT-01) for Gaganyaan conducted?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota. On August 24, 2025, ISRO successfully conducted the first Integrated Air Drop Test (IADT-01) for the Gaganyaan program at Sriharikota. The test validated the parachute-based deceleration system of the Crew Module in a typical mission scenario. Using a Chinook helicopter, a simulated Crew Module (~4.8 tons) was dropped from 3 km, deploying ten parachutes in sequence to reduce terminal velocity to 8 m/s for safe splashdown. The module was recovered by INS Anvesha. The test involved ISRO, DRDO, Indian Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard, ensuring mission readiness.

questions.question: Recently, which country officially joined the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Nepal Recently, Nepal officially became a member of the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA), a global initiative dedicated to the conservation of big cats and their habitats. This membership reinforces Nepal’s commitment to wildlife protection, biodiversity preservation, and combating threats such as poaching and habitat loss. By joining IBCA, Nepal will collaborate with other member nations on research, policy frameworks, and conservation strategies, aiming to strengthen global efforts for the survival of iconic big cat species like tigers, lions, and leopards.

questions.question: Women’s Equality Day 2025 marks the anniversary of which amendment that granted women voting rights?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 19th. Women’s Equality Day is observed in the U.S. on August 26, commemorating the 19th Amendment (1920) that granted women the right to vote. It celebrates historic progress in women’s rights while highlighting ongoing challenges, including wage gaps, underrepresentation in leadership, healthcare inequities, and educational barriers. The day is marked with community events, workshops, award ceremonies, and youth programs. Women’s Equality Day 2025 encourages learning about the suffrage movement, supporting women-led initiatives, amplifying women’s voices, and advocating for continued gender equality and empowerment.

questions.question: Who will lead India’s delegation to the 68th Commonwealth Parliamentary Conference in Barbados?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Lok Sabha Speaker. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla will lead India’s delegation to the 68th Commonwealth Parliamentary Conference (CPC) in Bridgetown, Barbados, from October 5–12, 2025. The conference, organized by the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA), gathers lawmakers from 180 branches to discuss democracy, governance, and cooperation. The Indian delegation includes Rajya Sabha Deputy Chairman, state presiding officers, and CPA India representatives. Delegates will participate in thematic workshops on democratic institutions, technology, climate, financial transparency, and youth empowerment, showcasing India’s parliamentary practices and promoting international collaboration.

questions.question: With reference to recent news, which cricket legend’s statue was unveiled by MCA at Wankhede Stadium?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Sunil Gavaskar. The Mumbai Cricket Association (MCA) unveiled a statue of Sunil Gavaskar and inaugurated the Sharad Pawar Cricket Museum at Wankhede Stadium on 23 August 2025. The bronze statue of Gavaskar celebrates his cricketing legacy, featuring his iconic shot to reach 10,000 Test runs. The museum honors Sharad Pawar’s contributions as an administrator, showcasing cricket artifacts, Mumbai’s Kanga League archives, and local cultural elements. The initiative preserves Mumbai’s cricket heritage, inspires young players, and highlights both sporting excellence and effective governance in Indian cricket.

questions.question: Who has been recently appointed Vice-President and Chief Risk Officer of NDB?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Rajiv Ranjan. Dr. Rajiv Ranjan, former Member of India’s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), has been appointed Vice-President and Chief Risk Officer of the BRICS-led New Development Bank (NDB) for a five-year term starting August 2025. With over 35 years of experience at the RBI, including roles as Executive Director and Head of Monetary Policy, he brings extensive expertise in economic research, policy formulation, and risk management. At NDB, he will oversee risk frameworks, credit and market risk assessment, and ensure financial and operational resilience across member nations.

questions.question: With reference to recent reports, Goldman Sachs projects that India’s household financial savings will generate how much cumulative inflows into financial assets by 2035?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. $9.5 trillion. Goldman Sachs projects that India’s household financial savings will generate $9.5 trillion in cumulative inflows into financial assets by 2035, reflecting a shift from physical assets to financial instruments. Household savings are expected to average 13% of GDP, up from 11.6% over the past decade. Major inflows will include insurance, pensions, bank deposits, equities, and mutual funds. This trend will strengthen domestic capital for corporate growth, develop long-term bond markets, boost retail investment, and enhance financial inclusion and wealth management in India.

questions.question: With reference to recent reports, Oman has set a target to attract how many Indian tourists annually?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 1 million. Oman has set a target to attract one million Indian tourists annually, aiming to strengthen tourism and economic ties with India. Building on 700,000 Indian visitors in 2024, the initiative focuses on luxury travel, destination weddings, MICE events, and adventure tourism. India has been identified as a priority market due to strong cultural, economic, and diaspora connections. Oman is enhancing connectivity, easing visa processes, and promoting the country through campaigns like ‘Focus Oman’ to ensure seamless travel and boost visitor engagement.

questions.question: Which ministry/department released India’s first national guidelines for animal blood banks?
options: Department of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying. India has released its first national guidelines for animal blood banks, issued on 25 August 2025 by the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying. The framework ensures standardized veterinary blood transfusion with donor screening, blood typing, biosafety, and ethical practices under a voluntary donation model. States will establish regulated blood banks, supported by a digital network for inventory and donor registries. Integrated into veterinary education, the initiative strengthens One Health, safeguards animal welfare, supports rural livelihoods, and enhances India’s agrarian economy.

questions.question: Which of the following statements related to the Constitution of India is true?
options: Fundamental Rights set social and economic democracy, while Directive Principles establish political democracy.
Fundamental Rights are moral and political sanctions, but Directive Principles are legal sanctions.
Fundamental Rights are justiciable, whereas Directive Principles of State are non-justiciable.
Fundamental Rights promote the welfare of the community, while Directive Principles promote individual welfare.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Fundamental Rights are justiciable, whereas Directive Principles of State are non-justiciable. Fundamental Rights are enforceable by courts and can be legally claimed by individuals, ensuring protection of basic human rights. In contrast, Directive Principles of State Policy, while guiding the government in creating policies for social and economic welfare, are non-justiciable, meaning they are not legally enforceable in courts. They serve as fundamental guidelines for governance but do not carry a direct legal sanction like Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India was amended in 2002 to insert a clause ‘k’, stating that a parent or guardian is required to provide opportunities for education to their child or ward between the ages of six and fourteen years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Article 51 A. This amendment made it a fundamental duty for parents and guardians to ensure that their children receive an education between the ages of six and fourteen. The 86th Amendment also: • Provided the right to education for children ages six to fourteen • Provided early childhood care until the age of six • Replaced Article 45 of the Directive principles of State policy The Constitution (Forty-second Amendment) Act of 1976 originally added Article 51A to the Constitution, which outlined 11 fundamental duties for citizens.

questions.question: Which amendments of the Constitution of India are aimed at strengthening local governments and ensuring an element of uniformity in their structure and functioning across the country?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2, i.e. 73rd and 74th. The 73rd and 74th Amendments of the Constitution, passed in 1992, aimed at strengthening local governments in India by providing constitutional status to Panchayats (rural local bodies) and Municipalities (urban local bodies). These amendments introduced uniformity in their structure and functioning across the country, ensuring democratic elections, the reservation of seats for women and marginalized communities, and the devolution of powers and responsibilities to local bodies. • 73rd Amendment: Deals with Panchayats. • 74th Amendment: Deals with Municipalities.

questions.question: The Fundamental Duties were added in the Indian Constitution by the recommendation of which of the following committees?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4, i.e. Swaran Singh Committee. The Fundamental Duties were added to the Indian Constitution through the 42nd Amendment in 1976, based on the recommendations of the Swaran Singh Committee. This committee, formed in 1976, recommended the inclusion of a set of duties for citizens to promote responsible participation in the democratic process and to uphold the values of the Constitution. These duties are enshrined in Article 51A and serve as a reminder to citizens of their responsibilities toward the nation, its integrity, and its cultural heritage.

questions.question: According to clause (2) of __________of the Constitution of India, the President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Article 124. According to Clause (2) of Article 124 of the Constitution of India, the President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India (CJI). This clause states that the President shall appoint the Chief Justice and other Supreme Court judges after consultation with the Supreme Court and the High Courts judges, as deemed necessary. The appointment process ensures independence and integrity in the judiciary, with the CJI traditionally appointed based on seniority among Supreme Court judges.

questions.question: Who set up the task force on Supportive Policy and Regulatory Framework for Micro Finance in India in 1999?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. NABARD. The task force on Supportive Policy and Regulatory Framework for Micro Finance in India in 1999 was set up by NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development). This initiative aimed to assess the microfinance sector and suggest a conducive policy and regulatory environment to promote its growth and outreach, especially in rural areas.

questions.question: Why was the growth rate of India during 1901-1921 stagnant and the phase of India’s population considered stationary?
options: Birth rate was low and death rate was high
Birth rate and death rate were high
Birth rate was high and death rate was low
Birth rate and death rate were low
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Birth rate and death rate were high • India's population growth rate between 1901 and 1921 is referred to as the period of stagnant or stationary phase, as the population growth rate was extremely low and almost constant. • Poor health and Medical services, widespread illiteracy, and inadequate distribution systems for food and other necessities were the primary causes for the high rates of birth and death during this time period.

questions.question: The industrial sector of the country was divided into how many schedules under the Industrial Policy Resolution 1956?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Three • Under the Industrial Policy Resolution (IPR), 1956, the Indian industrial sector was classified into three schedules to define the role of the public and private sectors: • Schedule A: 17 industries were exclusively reserved for the State (Central Government). These included sectors of national importance like atomic energy, defense production, railways, and heavy machinery. • Schedule B: 12 industries where both public and private sectors could participate, but the government had a dominant role. Examples include fertilizers, road transport, and machine tools. • Schedule C: All other industries not mentioned in A or B were left to the private sector, with the State playing a regulatory role.

questions.question: What was the approximate total budget outlay of the First Five-Year Plan in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Rs.2,069 crore. The First Five-Year Plan of India (1951-1956) had an approximate total budget outlay of ₹2,069 crore. This plan primarily emphasized agriculture, irrigation, and power to improve food production and rural infrastructure. Major projects like the Bhakra-Nangal Dam and Hirakud Dam were initiated to boost irrigation and energy supply. The plan achieved a growth rate of 3.6%, surpassing the target of 2.1%. It played a crucial role in strengthening India's economic foundation and self-sufficiency in food production.

questions.question: Which of the following incomes is NOT included in the factor income?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Scholarship of a student. The scholarship of a student is not included in factor income because it is a transfer income, not earned through participation in the production process. It is a one-way payment made without any productive service in return. Factor incomes are payments received for providing factors of production (land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship).

questions.question: The Brahmo Samaj was founded in 1828 by who among the following social reformers of British India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Raja Ram Mohan Roy. The Brahmo Samaj was founded in 1828 by Raja Ram Mohan Roy, one of the most prominent social reformers of British India. He established the Brahmo Samaj in Kolkata as a reformist movement within Hinduism, promoting monotheism and opposing idol worship, caste discrimination, and social evils like sati. The Samaj aimed to purify religion and promote rational thinking, education, and women’s rights. Raja Ram Mohan Roy’s efforts through the Brahmo Samaj laid the foundation for modern Indian society by encouraging religious tolerance, social equality, and progressive values that influenced future reform movements during the Indian renaissance.

questions.question: ____ were built to enshrine relics of Buddha and Buddhist saints, or to commemorate events of religious significance.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Stupas • Stupas were built to enshrine relics of Buddha and Buddhist saints or to commemorate events of religious significance. • The concept of stupas predates Buddhism and originally existed as burial mounds, or tumuli, used for interring śramaņas (ascetics) in a seated position. • The word "stupa" is derived from the Sanskrit term meaning "heap" or "pile." • In Buddhist tradition, this early form was adopted and gradually transformed into a more defined architectural structure, serving as a monument to house sacred relics and commemorate significant events.

questions.question: Name the Sikh Guru who developed the Gurmukhi script for writing the Punjabi language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Guru Angad. • Guru Angad, the second Guru of Sikhism, is credited with developing the Gurmukhi script in the 16th century. • He devised this script primarily to standardize the writing of Punjabi, making it more accessible to the people. Before Gurmukhi, various scripts were used, including Shahmukhi (a script derived from Persian) and other regional variations. • Guru Angad's main goal was to create a script that was simple and efficient, one that could be easily learned and used by the common people. Gurmukhi became a unifying script for the Sikh community and was particularly useful for the preservation and dissemination of the Sikh scriptures, such as the Guru Granth Sahib. • The introduction of Gurmukhi was a significant milestone in Sikh culture and literature, marking a pivotal moment in the development of Punjabi as a written language. • While Guru Angad’s contribution to the script was monumental, the script itself was later expanded and further popularized by subsequent Sikh Gurus, especially Guru Arjan, who compiled the Adi Granth in Gurmukhi.

questions.question: Which of the following dynasties is not associated with the southern part of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Chauhan (Chahaman) dynasty. • The Chauhan dynasty was associated with northern India, particularly with the regions of Rajasthan and Delhi. This dynasty is most famous for rulers like Prithviraj Chauhan, who ruled over Delhi and Ajmer in the 12th century. • In contrast, the Cheras, Pandyas, and Cholas were prominent dynasties in southern India: • The Chera dynasty ruled over Kerala and parts of Tamil Nadu. • The Pandya dynasty was based in southern Tamil Nadu, with its capital at Madurai. • The Chola dynasty was a major power in southern India, with its capital at Thanjavur, and it is known for its military conquests and cultural achievements. Thus, the Chauhan dynasty is not associated with southern India.

questions.question: The Buddhist Council that led to the final division in Buddhism into two parts, Hinayana and Mahayana, was held at which of the following sites?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Kashmir. The Buddhist Council that led to the final division of Buddhism into Hinayana and Mahayana was held during the Fourth Buddhist Council at Kundalvan (Kashmir) in the 1st century CE. It was convened under the patronage of Kanishka, the Kushan emperor. This council marked a significant split due to differences in beliefs and practices. Mahayana Buddhism emphasized the role of the Bodhisattva and compassion, while Hinayana focused on individual salvation and strict adherence to the original teachings of Buddha.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a peak of the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Guru Shikhar. Guru Shikhar is not a peak of the Himalayas. It is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range, located in Rajasthan, near Mount Abu. It stands at an elevation of about 1,722 meters and is a popular tourist and pilgrimage site. Other Options: • Kanchenjunga: The third-highest mountain in the world, located on the border between India (Sikkim) and Nepal. • Dhaulagiri: The seventh-highest mountain in the world, located in Nepal, part of the Himalayan range. • Makalu: The fifth-highest mountain in the world, also located in Nepal, within the Himalayas.

questions.question: Rabi crops are generally harvested in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. March-June. • Rabi Crops: Rabi crops are those crops that are sown in winter and harvested in summer. • Sowing Time: These crops are generally sown in the months of October-November, when the weather is cooler. • Harvesting Time: Rabi crops are harvested between March and June. They require a cool climate to grow and are harvested during the warmer months. • Major Rabi Crops: Major rabi crops include wheat, barley, chickpeas, peas, mustard, and pulses. • Role of Climate: Rabi crops grow better in cooler weather and require less water, making them suitable for the winter-spring season. These crops typically yield higher production depending on the climatic conditions.

questions.question: Approximately, what percentage of the world’s mangrove forests does India possess?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 7%. • India possesses approximately 7% of the world's total mangrove forests. • These forests are mainly located in coastal regions like West Bengal, Odisha, Gujarat, and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. • Mangroves act as natural barriers against storms and coastal erosion. • They support biodiversity, provide habitats for marine species, and contribute to carbon sequestration, enhancing environmental sustainability.

questions.question: According to the census of India 2011, what is the female literacy rate in Dadra and Nagar Haveli?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 64.32%. The female literacy rate in Dadra and Nagar Haveli, as per the 2011 Census of India, was 64.32%. This means that nearly two-thirds of women in the union territory were considered literate at the time. The overall literacy rate in Dadra and Nagar Haveli was 76.34%, with a higher male literacy rate of 85.17% compared to the female rate. Other Options: • 60.24%: This figure is closer to the female literacy rate of Chhattisgarh (60.24%) as per the 2011 Census, not Dadra and Nagar Haveli. • 75.93%: This is the female literacy rate of Himachal Pradesh (75.93%) according to the 2011 Census, not Dadra and Nagar Haveli. • 69.68%: This represents the female literacy rate of Gujarat (69.68%) as per the 2011 Census, not Dadra and Nagar Haveli. The 2011 Census highlights progress in female literacy, though regional disparities persist.

questions.question: The ‘Aman Setu’ is located between:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Srinagar and Muzaffarabad. • The ‘Aman Setu’ is located between Srinagar and Muzaffarabad. • Aman Setu is also known as the "Bridge of Peace, connects Srinagar (in Jammu and Kashmir, India) with Muzaffarabad (in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir) across the Line of Control (LoC). • The Aman Setu served as an important transit point for families separated by the partition of India and Pakistan in 1947. • This was used for humanitarian and cross-border travel purposes like the Srinagar–Muzaffarabad bus service

questions.question: Substances which have tendency to move from stronger to the weaker part of the external magnetic field are called ________,
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Diamagnetic. Diamagnetic substances are those which tend to move from the stronger to the weaker part of an external magnetic field. This behavior arises because these substances develop induced magnetic fields in the opposite direction to the applied magnetic field, resulting in a repulsive effect. Examples include bismuth, copper, silver, and gold. In contrast: • Paramagnetic substances move towards the stronger part of the magnetic field. • Ferromagnetic substances show strong attraction to magnetic fields and can retain magnetism.

questions.question: What is the gravitational force exerted by a hollow spherical shell of uniform density on a point mass located inside it?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Zero. The gravitational force exerted by a hollow spherical shell of uniform density on a point mass inside it is zero. This is a consequence of Newton's Shell Theorem, which states: • Inside the Shell: The gravitational forces from all parts of the shell cancel out symmetrically, resulting in no net force on the point mass. • Outside the Shell: The shell behaves as if all its mass were concentrated at its center. This theorem applies only to hollow spheres (or shells) with uniform density. For solid spheres, the force inside varies with distance from the center.

questions.question: In which algae are gametes flagellated and similar in size?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e Ulothrix. • Ulothrix is a green algae found in marine and fresh waters. Each cell contains a distinct nucleus, a central vacuole and a large thin chloroplast having one pyrenoid. • In most species, all the cells can form reproductive bodies. Ulothrix reproduces vegetatively by fragmentation, asexually by nonmotile resting spores and sexually by biflagellate gametes.

questions.question: Which of the following plants has 'hidden reproductive organs'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Marsilea. Marsilea is a type of ferns with "hidden reproductive organs." It is an aquatic plant that reproduces through sporocarps (reproductive structures), which are hidden within the soil or water. The plant produces spores instead of seeds, and the sporocarps contain these spores, which are not immediately visible. Unlike flowering plants, Marsilea does not have conspicuous reproductive organs such as flowers. Its method of reproduction is asexual and involves the release of spores from these concealed structures.

questions.question: Reproducing new plants by cells instead of vegetative parts or seeds is called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. tissue culture. • Tissue culture is a method of reproducing new plants by using cells, tissues, or organs instead of traditional vegetative parts or seeds. • In this technique, plant cells are cultured in a sterile, nutrient-rich medium under controlled conditions, allowing them to develop into complete plants. • This process is widely used in plant research, agriculture, and horticulture for cloning plants, conserving rare species, and producing plants with desirable traits.

questions.question: Which of the following internal parasites primarily affects the respiratory system of birds?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Gapeworms. • The trachea and bronchi are the entry points for gapeworms, which, like Syngamidae nematodes, clog the airways and cause respiratory distress in birds. • The term "gapeworms" refers to the breathing difficulties, gasping, and gaping behavior caused by this impediment. • Inflammation and excessive mucus production are caused by the irritation of the respiratory tract caused by these parasites, which exacerbates respiratory distress. • The entire health and performance of birds can be negatively impacted by severe infestations that impair their oxygen intake. • To reduce the effects of gapeworm infestations on chicken flocks, effective management and treatment are essential.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of Beryllium?
options: Has a low melting and boiling point
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Forms covalent compounds Beryllium (Be) forms covalent compounds due to its small size, high ionization energy, and significant polarizing power, which distorts the electron cloud of anions, favoring covalent bonding. For example, BeCl₂ is covalent in nature. Other Options: • Has a low melting and boiling point – Incorrect. Beryllium has a high melting point (1287°C) due to its strong metallic bonding. • Reacts with water – Incorrect. Unlike alkaline earth metals (e.g., Ca, Mg), Beryllium does not react with water due to its oxide layer passivation. • Forms ionic compounds – Partially correct. While Be can form some ionic compounds, its strong polarizing nature favors covalent bonding, unlike other Group 2 elements that predominantly form ionic compounds. Thus, the most accurate property of Beryllium is its tendency to form covalent compounds.

questions.question: The substances whose odour changes in acidic or basic media are called _________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Olfactory indicators. • An Olfactory indicator is a substance whose smell varies depending on whether it is mixed with an acidic or basic solution. Olfactory indicators can be used in the laboratory to test whether a solution is a base or an acid, a process called olfactory titration. Onion, clove oil and vanilla extract are examples. • Natural Indicator is a type of indicator that can be found naturally and can determine whether the substance is an acidic substance or a basic substance. • A synthetic indicator is a man-made chemical substance used to determine pH. • Acid-base indicators are chemicals used to determine whether an aqueous solution is acidic, neutral, or alkaline.

questions.question: What is the latest version of MS Word?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Word 2019. • The latest version of Microsoft Office is Office 2019. • Microsoft released the Office 2019 for Windows and Mac on September 24, 2018. The Windows version runs only on Windows 10. • Microsoft Word or MS Word (often called Word) is a graphical word processing program that users can type with. • It is made by the computer company Microsoft. Its purpose is to allow users to type and save documents.

questions.question: Which of the following is a program that makes the computer easier to use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. utility. Understanding • The utility is a program that makes the computer easier to use. • Utility software is software that helps to maintain and protect the computer system. • It does not directly interface with the hardware. • System utilities are the core software functions that allow us to manage computers. • Functions of System Utility: • Disk Cleanup • Disk Defragmentation • System Restore • Disk Compression • Antivirus scan

questions.question: Microcomputer's hardware consists of three basic categories of physical equipment i.e. __________.
options: system unit, primary storage, secondary storage
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. system unit, input/output, memory. • Microcomputer hardware consists of three basic categories of the physical equipment i.e. system unit, input/output, memory. • A microcomputer is a computer that uses a microprocessor as its central processing unit. • It is physically smaller in size as compared to mainframe and minicomputers. • Microcomputers are easier to use and also inexpensive.

questions.question: What does IRC stand for?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Internet Relay Chat. • IRC stands for Internet Relay Chat. • It is a protocol for real-time text messaging between internet-connected computers created in 1988. • The chat process works on a client/server networking model. • It is mainly used for group discussion in chat rooms called “channels” although it supports private messages between two users, data transfer, and various server-side and client-side commands.

questions.question: Which festival, marked by the 'Chingam Valzakkal' feast, showcases grand processions of decorated elephants and traditional 'Puli Kali' performances?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Onam. • Onam is a traditional harvest festival predominantly celebrated in Kerala, India. • It features the ‘Chingam Valzakkal’ feast, a grand meal served during the celebrations. • One of the key highlights is ‘Puli Kali’, where performers painted like tigers dance in the streets. • Celebrated in the month of Chingam (Malayalam calendar), Onam reflects Kerala’s rich cultural heritage. Explanation of other options: Bihu: This is a harvest festival from Assam. Pongal: This is a harvest festival from Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian festivals is celebrated to pay reverence to Lord Jagannath?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Ratha Yatra. The Ratha Yatra (Chariot Festival) is a world-renowned Hindu festival celebrated with immense devotion and grandeur, primarily in Puri, Odisha. This festival is solely dedicated to Lord Jagannath, his elder brother Balabhadra, and sister Subhadra. Explanation of other Options: Thrissur Pooram: This is a spectacular annual temple festival celebrated in Thrissur, Kerala. Hemis: This is a famous annual festival celebrated in the Hemis Monastery in Ladakh. Losar: This is the Tibetan New Year festival, celebrated by Buddhists in Tibet, Nepal, Bhutan, and parts of India like Ladakh, Arunachal Pradesh, and Sikkim.

questions.question: Of which among the following states, the "Dandiya Ras" dance belongs?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Gujarat. The Dandiya Ras dance belongs to Gujarat. It is a vibrant, energetic folk dance performed during Navratri, where dancers use decorated sticks (dandiyas) to rhythmic beats. The dance celebrates the victory of good over evil and is deeply rooted in Gujarati culture. Other Options: • Kerala – Known for Kathakali and Mohiniyattam, classical dance forms, not Dandiya. • Haryana – Famous for Ghoomar and Jhumar, but not Dandiya. • Himachal Pradesh – Known for Nati, a traditional dance, unrelated to Dandiya. Thus, Gujarat is the correct answer as Dandiya Ras is an integral part of its cultural heritage.

questions.question: To which among the following states does Mohiniyattam dance form belong?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Kerala. Mohiniyattam is a classical dance form that belongs to Kerala. It is characterized by graceful, swaying movements, elegant gestures, and expressive storytelling, often performed by women. The dance is inspired by the mythological enchantress Mohini, an avatar of Lord Vishnu, and reflects Kerala's cultural heritage through its music (Carnatic style) and traditional attire. Other Options: • Uttar Pradesh: Known for Kathak, a dance form marked by intricate footwork and spins. • Punjab: Famous for Bhangra and Gidda, energetic folk dances celebrating harvest and festivals. • Tamil Nadu: Home to Bharatanatyam, a classical dance with structured poses and rhythmic movements.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian composer to win the Grammy Award?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Pandit Ravi Shankar. In 1968, the late legendary Indian composer Pandit Ravi Shankar became the first Indian composer to win an Grammy for his album West meets East in the cateogry- 'Best Chamber Music Performance' . Later in 1973, he again won a Grammy for his album- The Concert for Bangladesh, in the category Album of the Year. He he also received a Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award and the first Indian to do so. Explanation of Other Options: • Zubin Mehta, a renowned orchestral conductor, has won 5 Grammy Awards and has been nominated multiple times, with his first win in 1981 and his last in 2008. • Zakir Hussain, the celebrated tabla maestro, won four Grammy Awards, including three in one night at the 66th Grammy Awards in 2024, becoming the first Indian musician to do so. • A.R. Rahman won two Academy Awards in 2009 for Slumdog Millionaire—Best Original Score and Best Original Song ("Jai Ho")—and two Grammy Awards in 2010 for Best Compilation Soundtrack Album and Best Song Written for Visual Media, also for the same film.

questions.question: Which of the following is the autobiography of the first Indian President, Dr. Rajendra Prasad?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Atmakatha. • Atmakatha is the autobiography of Dr. Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India. • It was written in Hindi and provides insight into his personal life, freedom struggle involvement, and his contributions to the Indian independence movement. Other options: • A Shot at a Story – A collection of short stories by J. R. Ackerley, not related to Dr. Prasad. • My Country My Life – Autobiography of L. K. Advani, senior BJP leader.

questions.question: The term ‘Follow – On’ is used in which of the following games/sports?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Cricket. The term "Follow-On" is used in the sport of cricket. It refers to a situation in a multi-day match (such as a Test match or a first-class match) where the team batting second scores significantly fewer runs than the team that batted first. If the team batting first has a lead of a specified number of runs (usually 200 runs in Test matches), they can ask the team batting second to bat again immediately, instead of the first team batting for their second innings. This is called enforcing the follow-on.

questions.question: The term 'Straight drive' is related to which of the following sports?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Cricket. • The straight drive is a key shot in a batsman’s repertoire, requiring good technique, timing, and a steady stance. • It is both a defensive and an attacking shot, allowing the batsman to score runs while maintaining a low risk of dismissal. This shot epitomizes classical cricketing elegance and skill.
